acceptance_targets: [ch14-craft-01] At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:73, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:139, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:249, at least two of the three beats must show the joke dying through on-page behavior alone; fail if more than one targeted beat still tells the reader it would have landed on another morning or in another house.

[ch14-prose-01] Across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:61, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:225, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:331, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:373, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:411, no more than one beat may still rely on the explicit negation-and-correction template; the other revised beats must communicate the same emotional turn through directly readable action, bodily sensation, or image.

findings.acceptance_test: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:73, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:139, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:249, at least two of the three beats must show the joke dying through on-page behavior alone; fail if more than one targeted beat still tells the reader it would have landed on another morning or in another house.
findings.problem: The chapter repeatedly explains that a line might have been funny on another day instead of letting failed humor register through silence, body language, or changed behavior. That tonal scorekeeping blunts the deadened comic pressure the scene has already earned and makes the narration feel protective of its own wit.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the tonal aftermath beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:73, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:139, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:249 so the joke failure is conveyed by observable response in the room: a missed laugh, a dropped gaze, resumed labor, or another concrete pressure cue. Cut or replace the explanatory 'would have/might have been funny' framing rather than paraphrasing it.

findings.acceptance_test: Across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:61, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:225, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:331, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:373, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:411, no more than one beat may still rely on the explicit negation-and-correction template; the other revised beats must communicate the same emotional turn through directly readable action, bodily sensation, or image.
findings.problem: A repeated negation-and-correction sentence habit starts to flatten the chapter's emotional beats: the narration keeps telling the reader what did not happen or which softer reaction did not occur before naming the real feeling. Used this often, the move stops feeling precise and starts feeling like autopilot emphasis.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated negation beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:61, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:225, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:331, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:373, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_14.md:411 so at least most of them render the pressure directly through sensation, action, or image instead of through 'did not' or 'that was worse' scaffolding. Keep one such inversion at most where it buys a distinct effect; vary the rest.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into the book's strange civic biology, vulgar humor, erotic consequence, and class abrasion. When a choice appears between the cleaner version and the truer, messier one, choose the messier version so long as causality and character truth remain exact. Treat the city's slime, smell, heat, and appetite as part of the novel's beauty rather than embarrassment to be polished away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If sex, shame, hunger, class humiliation, bodily mess, or economic pressure starts drifting into decorous vagueness, rewrite until the scene carries concrete sensory and social cost on the page.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm across the novel; uncontracted forms should signal emphasis, class performance, ceremonial language, or Gideon's deliberate control.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate crowd scenes with cleanup, ritual with labor, public humiliation with private tenderness, and single-sweep chapters with multi-scene accumulations; no two consecutive chapters should feel built from the same emotional weather, dialogue density, or scene rhythm.
prose_style_profile.diction: Period-aware but not museum-stiff; vivid, bodily, class-conscious, and willing to be ugly or funny without slipping into arch parody or modern snark.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person anchored to Silas Vale for the full novel, with no head-hopping and no neutral historian voice; Inez remains legible through observed action, dialogue, and the objects she shares.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Elastic and pressure-sensitive: quick comic snap in public scenes, thicker sensory accumulation in labor and erotic passages, and hard clean sentences at moments of shame or decision.
